claims.evidence_text: Winter wheat and triticale can also survive, but only if seeded early by mid-late August, and they generally produce less biomass and survive fewer extreme cold events of -15o C (Martens & Entz,30 2011; Blackshaw 2008).
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat (as an overwintering cover) survives in Manitoba only if seeded early by mid-late August.

claims.evidence_text: Winter wheat and triticale can also survive, but only if seeded early by mid-late August, and they generally produce less biomass and survive fewer extreme cold events of -15o C (Martens & Entz,30 2011; Blackshaw 2008).
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat survives fewer extreme cold events around -15 °C compared with rye.

claims.evidence_text: comparable to spring wheat tolerating –8 to –10 °C at the seedling stage
claims.claim_summary: Spring wheat tolerates -8 to -10 °C at the seedling stage.
